title: RepoBrief-Dateisystemzugriffe dirfd-gebunden härten
goal: Harden consumer-local RepoBrief publication against hostile or accidental replacement of parent path components by binding traversal, creation, verification and replacement to trusted directory file descriptors with a portable fail-closed fallback policy.

acceptance.assertion: The supported attacker and concurrency model is explicit, including which path-component replacement attacks are in scope and which platforms are supported.
Security-sensitive traversal, file creation, verification and atomic replacement are anchored to trusted directory descriptors using openat/openat2-equivalent semantics where supported.
Platforms without the required primitives either use a documented equally safe implementation for the declared threat model or fail closed instead of silently weakening guarantees.
Deterministic tests exercise symlink swaps, parent-directory replacement, special files and concurrent renames without escaping the publication root or accepting mixed identities.
Focused adversarial tests, supported-platform tests, the complete Lenskit suite and GitHub CI pass on the exact implementation head.
The result does not claim kernel-bug resistance, network-filesystem equivalence, privilege-boundary isolation or security on untested platforms.
